vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

generated_parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g
Each internal_feature must have corresponding geometry_ops
Each interface has reciprocal definition in adjacent_parts
Critical joints have visible preload/locking evidence
All required evidence_features present in part_tree/geometry_ops

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex interface geometry.
risk.mit: Implement detailed assembly instructions and conduct pre-assembly checks.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance leading to jamming in snap-fit interfaces.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearance dimensions based on prototype testing results.

risk.desc: Insufficient service access might hinder maintenance operations.
risk.mit: Ensure all necessary access points are clearly marked and easily accessible.

risk.desc: Potential for thermal expansion issues affecting structural integrity.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal analysis and incorporate appropriate expansion joints.

risk.desc: Risk of overloading fuselage spine if wing spar loads exceed design limits.
risk.mit: Perform stress analysis and reinforce critical load-bearing areas.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Wing spar loads into fuselage spine via spar_socket
Avionics cooling airflow through service hole
Thermal insulation provided by TPU layer
V-tail motion transmitted to ruddervator
Landing gear compression force
Electrical harness passing through sensor pocket
Human access for camera inspection

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit

verification_focus: Clearances on bolted and snap-fit interfaces
Load path from wing spar to fuselage spine
Service access for camera and sensor maintenance

risk.desc: Potential misalignment due to tolerance stack-up in complex interfaces.
risk.mit: Implement tighter tolerances and conduct thorough alignment checks during assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity if CFRP layers are not properly bonded.
risk.mit: Conduct non-destructive testing (NDT) on CFRP layers to ensure proper bonding.

risk.desc: Possible failure of landing gear mount due to inadequate support.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the landing gear mount to provide better support.

risk.desc: Risk of nacelle attachment point failing under repeated loading.
risk.mit: Use higher-strength materials and reinforce the attachment points.

risk.desc: Potential for interference between moving parts if clearance is not maintained.
risk.mit: Ensure all critical interfaces have appropriate clearance fits.
